items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. It preserves the mock-heroic/theatrical register of «терзают смутные сомнения» and correctly renders the elliptical parallelism «У Шпака — …, у посла — …» as a punchy list rather than a plain possessive clause, keeping the comedic callback structure intact.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The translation keeps the repetitive, banal Soviet-style politeness and the absurd self-introduction loop that creates the era-clash humor.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The imperative and formal verb “demand” preserve the sudden authoritarian persona shift that drives the joke.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. It keeps the elevated, archaic-sounding melancholy and the socially jarring “old woman” address that produces the humor in a modern Soviet setting.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. It correctly makes “the demons” the agent (subject) and preserves the panicked repetition that sells the misunderstanding of modern technology as sorcery.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The elevated “Behold” and religious phrasing preserve the mock-solemn miracle claim that is central to the joke.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. It conveys the MC-like command opening the dance, matching the comedic ‘party decree’ energy without over-literal “Everyone dance!”

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. It keeps the breezy, self-satisfied criminal cheerfulness and the idiomatic feel (not a stiff literal “I came in successfully”).

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant item “caviar” in English, preserving the Soviet-status-symbol inversion joke and preventing audience confusion.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. It preserves the comic rhythm of obsessive inventory and the trailing self-correction/afterthought feel that signals Shpak’s exaggeration and status-posturing.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The calm, sing-song repetition is preserved, maintaining the darkly comic resignation and the shared ‘we’re all crazy’ punchline.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
